INT. KID'S ROOM - MODERN SUBURBAN DUPLEX - DAY

It's a bright and colorful child's space designed with simple, clean, softened high-tech lines. There are masses of toys everywhere, thousands of baseball cards, piles of comic books, a small mountain of games and educational programs next to a mini-computer system. A durable-looking TEN-YEAR-OLD BOY is at the window. He is setting up a telescope, fresh out of the box. He reads the instruction booklet as he points the telescope outside, beyond his small backyard, over palm trees, up toward the FULL MOON SHINING AGAINST AN AFTERNOON SKY. Now the Boy looks through his telescope. HIS P.O.V. Its mysterious whiteness shines against as he FINDS the moon. the light blue sky as he FOCUSES, CLOSES IN. THE BOY turns from his telescope, picks up it's instruction booklet, looks at diagrammed pictures of constellations . Soaring, spectacular, we TRAVEL IN space. The CAMERA MOVES FROM the P.O.V. of something moving very fast with a LOUD, LOUD ROAR, PAST sharply glittering stars, brilliant constellations shining out against the deep blue expanses of outer space. Faster and faster we GO, as the moon rises up INTO THE SCENE in the distance. Now the moon moves in fast, becomes closer and closer. We ZIP OVER the moon's surface now, WHIZ OVER crevices, dark stones, barren rock formations, huge craters. Now we PASS the moon, MOVE INTO a thicker starfield, TURN DOWNWARD fast.
"COCOON" - 8/2/84

THE EARTH moves INTO VIEW, tiny at first, growing in size faster and faster as we APPROACH its atmosphere, as the stars dim. Faster and faster we APPROACH the Earth. INTO thick clouds, lit with sunset. Then SKIMMING FAST OVER rapidly thinning cloud formations. Now we STOP SUDDENLY, HEAD STRAIGHT DOWN TOWARD the Earth, THROUGH the fog of more thick clouds. The Atlantic Ocean. pops his head out of the water, looks up at the sky. pop their heads up, stare up at the sky. Suddenly, bright lights shine from above, reflect off the Dolphins. dives underwater again. The other Dolphins follow. From underwater the Dolphins observe as a circular shape can barely be made out above the surface of the ocean. Now the light from above grows brighter and brighter, becomes a blinding glare that suddenly WHITES OUT THE SCREEN.
